bins.name: Storage enables load flexibility
bins.desc: Energy storage is uniquely positioned to make loads curtailable and flexible, enabling expedited interconnection by allowing large loads to avoid drawing during peak constraints and maintaining dispatchable energy during grid stress. This flexibility can be combined with permissive charging rules during low-usage intervals to unlock faster pathways for load deployment.
quotes: energy storage is uniquely situated to make an expedited interconnection process for large loads both feasible and reliable
Energy storage is uniquely positioned as a rapid pathway for making loads curtailable and flexible, enabling them to potentially seek and obtain expedited interconnection.

Enabling large loads to avoid drawing from the grid by maintaining dispatchable energy during peak demand events—when transmission constraints are most salient—can dramatically reduce or eliminate the need for network upgrades that would otherwise require years of study and construction

the Commission should ensure in any final action that storage resources are permitted to charge in low-usage intervals, when doing so will not trigger the need for expensive and long-lead-time upgrades

By advancing policies that allow storage deployment to keep pace with large load growth, the Commission can help stabilize electricity prices for consumers while strengthening America's energy and economic security.

bins.name: Storage reduces network upgrade costs
bins.desc: Storage-backed flexible loads can dramatically reduce or eliminate the need for expensive, long-lead-time network upgrades by enabling loads to avoid drawing during transmission constraints. Clear charging rules that permit storage to charge during low-usage periods without triggering upgrades unlock cost savings for both developers and consumers.
quotes: Enabling large loads to avoid drawing from the grid by maintaining dispatchable energy during peak demand events—when transmission constraints are most salient—can dramatically reduce or eliminate the need for network upgrades that would otherwise require years of study and construction

the Commission should ensure in any final action that storage resources are permitted to charge in low-usage intervals, when doing so will not trigger the need for expensive and long-lead-time upgrades

By advancing policies that allow storage deployment to keep pace with large load growth, the Commission can help stabilize electricity prices for consumers while strengthening America's energy and economic security.

bins.name: Joint study of storage and load
bins.desc: Storage resources should be studied jointly with large loads as hybrid facilities to enable expedited interconnection processes. Clear, objective standards for load flexibility criteria are needed to prevent inconsistent transmission provider implementation and ensure developers can reliably access expedited pathways.
quotes: energy storage is uniquely situated to make an expedited interconnection process for large loads both feasible and reliable
Energy storage is uniquely positioned as a rapid pathway for making loads curtailable and flexible, enabling them to potentially seek and obtain expedited interconnection.
Without objective criteria, transmission providers will apply inconsistent standards, and developers will face regulatory uncertainty that undermines the benefits of expedited processing.

bins.name: Expand hybrid facility definitions
bins.desc: FERC should explicitly include energy storage in the hybrid facility definition, consistent with the Large Generator Interconnection Procedures that already accommodate storage. This definition expansion is a threshold requirement to enable storage's role in expedited large load interconnection.
quotes: the Commission's Large Generator Interconnection Procedures accommodate energy storage, its definition of 'hybrid facilities' in the large load context should also include energy storage

bins.name: Develop capacity accreditation for long-duration storage
bins.desc: No organized market today accredits battery durations beyond 8 hours or hybrid resources combining long-duration storage with generation. FERC should direct RTOs/ISOs to develop capacity accreditation values for long-duration (10+ hour), multi-day (24+ hour), and hybrid storage resources to unlock their potential in supporting large load interconnection and grid reliability.
quotes: certain energy storage and hybrid resources are not accredited in capacity markets today. The Commission should ensure that all grid operators develop capacity accreditation values for long-duration energy storage (>10 hours in duration), multi-day energy storage (>24 hours in duration), and hybrid resources

bins.name: Establish clear load flexibility criteria
bins.desc: FERC should establish objective criteria for load flexibility (curtailment percentage, annual hours, response times, and whether commitments are firm or conditional) to ensure consistent transmission provider implementation. Without such standards, developers face regulatory uncertainty and cannot reliably access expedited processing benefits.
quotes: Without objective criteria, transmission providers will apply inconsistent standards, and developers will face regulatory uncertainty that undermines the benefits of expedited processing.
